@[ext]

    (k : ‚Ñï) (Œº ŒΩ : Measure ‚Ñù) (X : Œ© ‚Üí Matrix (Fin k) (Fin k) ‚Ñù) (P : Measure Œ©) where

@[simp]

  exact Y.meas

/-- The index set for the independent random variables of a Wigner matrix. -/
def Index (n : ‚Ñï) := { p : Fin n √ó Fin n // p.1 ‚â§ p.2 }

instance : Fintype (Index n) := by
  rw[Index]
  infer_instance

@[simp]
lemma off_diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n Œº ŒΩ X P) (i j : Fin n) (hij : i ‚âÝ j) :
  HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ i j) Œº P:= by
  -- Since $i \neq j$, we have either $i < j$ or $j < i$. We can handle these cases separately using the off_diag_dist and the symmetry of the Wigner matrix.
  by_cases h : j < i;
  ¬∑ -- Since $j < i$, we can apply the off_diag_dist condition directly.
    apply Y.off_diag_dist i j h
    all_goals unreachable!;
  ¬∑ -- Since $i < j$, we can apply the off_diag_dist hypothesis with $i$ and $j$ swapped.
    have h_swap : HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ j i) Œº P := by
      cases lt_or_gt_of_ne hij <;> aesop
      generalize_proofs at *;
      have := Y.off_diag_dist j i h_1; aesop;
    generalize_proofs at *;
    -- Since $X$ is symmetric, we have $X i j = X j i$.
    have h_symm : ‚àÄ œâ, X œâ i j = X œâ j i := by
      -- By the symmetry of the Wigner matrix, we have $X i j = X j i$ for all $i$ and $j$.
      intros œâ; exact Y.symm i j œâ
    generalize_proofs at *;
    aesop;

@[simp]
lemma diagonal_law (Y : isWignerMatrix n Œº ŒΩ X P) (i : Fin n) : HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ i i) ŒΩ P:= by
   apply Y.diag_dist i

lemma symmetric (Y : isWignerMatrix n Œº ŒΩ X P) : ‚àÄ (œâ : Œ©), (X œâ).IsSymm := by
  intro œâ
  apply Matrix.IsSymm.ext

lemma indep_entries (Y : isWignerMatrix n Œº ŒΩ X P) (i j k l : Fin n) (hdiff : Sym2.mk (i,j) ‚âÝ Sym2.mk (k,l)) :
    IndepFun (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ i j) (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ k l) P := by
  -- Define the projections from the Wigner matrix to its entries.

  have h_indep : iIndepFun (fun (p : {p : Fin n √ó Fin n // p.1 ‚â§ p.2}) ‚Ü¶ proj p) P := by

  HasLaw (fun œâ ‚Ü¶ X œâ i j) Œº P:= by

abbrev WignerSpace (n : ‚Ñï) := Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù

instance (n : ‚Ñï) : MeasurableSpace (WignerSpace n) := by
  infer_instance

/-- The measure distribution for a Wigner matrix. It assigns the measure `ŒΩ` to diagonal
entries and `Œº` to off-diagonal entries. -/
def wignerMeasureDist (n : ‚Ñï) : Index n ‚Üí Measure ‚Ñù :=
  fun (p : Index n) =>
    if p.val.1 = p.val.2 then ŒΩ
    else Œº


/-- The probability measure on the sample space `WignerSpace n`, which makes the
coordinate projections independent random variables with distributions given by
`wignerMeasureDist`. -/
noncomputable
def WignerMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : Measure (WignerSpace n) :=
  Measure.pi (wignerMeasureDist Œº ŒΩ n)

instance instIsProbabilityMeasure (n : ‚Ñï) : IsProbabilityMeasure (WignerMeasure Œº ŒΩ n) := by

/-- The function that takes an element of WignerSpace (a map Index n ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
to the corresponding function (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù that respects the symmetric structure of Wigner
matrices.-/
@[grind, simp]
def wignerMatrixEntryFunction (œâ : WignerSpace n) : (Fin n) ‚Üí (Fin n) ‚Üí ‚Ñù :=
  fun i j =>
    if h_eq : i = j then

    else if h_lt : i < j then

         apply le_of_not_lt h_lt
         apply ne_of_not_lt

      œâ (‚ü®(j, i), le_of_lt h_gt‚ü©)

def wignerMatrixMap' (œâ : WignerSpace n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMap (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

/-- A wigner matrix is defined as a matrix valued random variable, rescaled by 1/‚àön.-/
noncomputable
def wignerMatrixMapScaled (n : ‚Ñï) : (WignerSpace n) ‚Üí Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù :=
  fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ‚Ñù)) ‚Ä¢ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)

/--For any `(n : ‚Ñï)` the map `wignerMatrixMap` is measurable from `WignerSpace n` to
  `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ‚Ñù`-/
@[fun_prop]
lemma wignerMatrixMapMeasurable (n : ‚Ñï) : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
  simp [wignerMatrixMap, Matrix.of, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
  all_goals

The proof is remarkably concise! Let's explain why:
- `wignerMatrixMap` is defined as `fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ (wignerMatrixMap' œâ)`.
- `wignerMatrixMap'` is defined as `fun (œâ : WignerSpace n) ‚Ü¶ Matrix.of (wignerMatrixEntryFunction œâ)`.
- `wignerMatrixEntryFunction` is a function from `Fin n √ó Fin n` to `‚Ñù`.
- `Matrix.of` takes a function `Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and creates a matrix.
- The definition of `wignerMatrixEntryFunction` is such that it's a simple function from `Fin n √ó Fin n` (which is the index set) to `‚Ñù`. This function is clearly measurable because it's built from simple operations (if-then-else, comparisons, and projections).
- `Matrix.of` preserves the measurability of the input function.
- The composition of these functions is therefore measurable.

The `simp` tactic expands the definition of `wignerMatrixMap` and related functions, while `all_goals infer_instance` handles the remaining goals by finding appropriate instances in the library. This is a powerful combination in Lean 4 for proving the measurability of complex functions constructed from simpler ones.
